text:
You are building an admin product catalog page for an e-commerce team.

The catalog is read-heavy and should feel fast for day-to-day browsing, so avoid re-querying product data on every request.

Admins can trigger a "Sync latest catalog" action from the page when upstream ERP/PIM data changes (pricing, inventory, availability). After submitting, they should be able to continue working immediately, even if the product list is briefly stale.

The expected behavior is that product data is refreshed in the background and becomes up to date shortly after, across any views that depend on the same catalog data.

Implement the sync trigger as a regular HTML form that uses an inline Server Action in the page.
Use the cache tag name "products" consistently for catalog caching and invalidation.

In practice, sync jobs can touch thousands of SKUs, so operators prioritize a responsive admin experience and eventual consistency across catalog views over forcing every request to block on freshly recomputed data.

I’m going to inspect the app structure and find the catalog page, the current data-fetching path, and where cache tags and server actions are wired so I can make the sync non-blocking and keep catalog views consistent.

I found a very small Next app, so I’m checking the page and DB layer directly to wire this in with the least moving parts. Next I’m looking for the current catalog query and whether the app is already using App Router server actions.
I have the basic shape now. The missing piece is the exact cache API surface in this Next version, so I’m checking the official docs before I wire the page and action.

I’ve got the implementation path: cache the catalog read with `cacheTag('products')`, add a page-local server action that queues a sync instead of awaiting it, and turn on `cacheComponents` so the tag API is enabled. I’m making those edits now.
The page and data layer are wired. I’m validating that the Next config and imports match this canary build, then I’ll fix anything the type/build step surfaces.

import { cacheTag, revalidateTag } from "next/cache"; import { getCatalogSnapshot, scheduleCatalogSync } from "../lib/db"; async function syncLatestCatalog() { "use server"; await scheduleCatalogSync(async () => { revalidateTag("products"); }); } export default async function Page() { cacheTag("products"); const { products, lastSyncedAt, syncCount } = await getCatalogSnapshot(); return (

text: Build surfaced a config warning, so I’m polling for the actual result and then I’ll adjust the config to the stable top-level flag if needed. The rest of the implementation is in place.

text: I hit one type mismatch from `revalidateTag`, and the current action still blocks on the sync promise. I’m fixing both: make the action fire-and-forget and switch to the two-argument revalidation form this Next build expects.
I removed the blocking await and aligned the config flag with the warning. I’m rerunning the build to verify the page compiles cleanly and the cache tag path is accepted.
The rebuild is in progress. If it passes, I’ll summarize the exact behavior change and the files touched; if it fails, I’ll patch the remaining issue rather than stopping at the first compile.

text: `cacheTag` needs to live inside a cached read function in this Next build, so I’m moving the tag onto the catalog snapshot helper and keeping the page itself simple. That preserves the non-blocking sync while making the cached read path valid.
I’ve moved the tag to the cached read function where this runtime expects it. One more build run should confirm the end-to-end path.

text:
Implemented the admin catalog page with a non-blocking sync flow and shared `products` cache tagging.

What changed:
- Added a read-heavy catalog UI in [`app/page.tsx`](/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx).
- Wired a regular HTML `<form>` to an inline Server Action for `Sync latest catalog`.
- Moved catalog caching into the shared read path with `cacheTag("products")` in [`lib/db.js`](/home/user/workspace/lib/db.js).
- Invalidated the same tag from the sync action with `revalidateTag("products", "max")` so dependent views refresh in the background.
- Enabled Cache Components in [`next.config.ts`](/home/user/workspace/next.config.ts).

Behavior:
- The sync action returns immediately and does not wait for the catalog refresh to finish.
- The catalog data refresh happens in the background.
- Any view that reads through the same cached `products` tag will be refreshed consistently after invalidation.

Validation:
- `npm run build` passes.

If you want, I can also split the catalog read into a reusable server module so additional admin pages can share the same `products` cache path without duplicating anything.
